issue_body:
Home Assistant release with the issue:
0.106

Operating environment (HassOS/Generic):
Hass.io

Description of problem:
When people try to login it doesnt work. It seems like Toonalib (do i say that right) have to be changed because toon developers build in a new second login. 
Integration:
https://www.home-assistant.io/integrations/toon/
https://github.com/home-assistant/home-assistant.io/blob/current/source/_integrations/toon.markdown

I'm not the only one with this problem. People who are logged in already seems to have data but new users / users who needed to login again got a error:
Login is invalid. 

Pls fix.
ps, i hope i'm on the right place for this issue? newbie so sorry if the place is wrong.

This is the only reason i'm not yet been Nabu Casa-account member. I would like to pay 5 dollars a month if Toon is working.

regards, 
Tim.
pr_body:
<!--
  You are amazing! Thanks for contributing to our project!
  Please, DO NOT DELETE ANY TEXT from this template! (unless instructed).
-->
## Breaking change
<!--
  If your PR contains a breaking change for existing users, it is important
  to tell them what breaks, how to make it work again and why we did this.
  This piece of text is published with the release notes, so it helps if you
  write it towards our users, not us.
  Note: Remove this section if this PR is NOT a breaking change.
-->

The Toon integration has been rewritten to support the new authentication methods from Eneco/Electrabel and Viesgo. After upgrading, go to the integrations panel, to re-authenticate the integration.

The `scan_interval` YAML configuration option has been deprecated. It is no longer used as this integration is now push-based. If you have it configured, please remove it from your configuration.

The "Holiday Mode" & "Toon Program" binary sensors have been replaced by a "Holiday Mode" and "Thermostat Program" switch, thus having new entities. You may need to adjust your automations if you used those in that.

Service `toon.update` has been removed, it is no longer needed as this is now a push-based integration. Please make sure you remove those from your automations.


## Proposed change
<!-- 
  Describe the big picture of your changes here to communicate to the
  maintainers why we should accept this pull request. If it fixes a bug
  or resolves a feature request, be sure to link to that issue in the
  additional information section.
-->

This PR rewrites the integration fully. The main reasoning for this is the new authentication used by Quby (the manufacturer). The current integration is broken as of last week.

Issues/features in this PR (HA perspective):

- OAuth2 authentication for Eneco (Toon), Electrabel (Boxx) and Viesgo (product is white-labeled)
- Full asynchronous implementation
- Adds switches for binary sensors that should have been a switch
- Convert this integration from Pull to Push (Pull = fallback), using webhooks (support both local and Nabu Casa Cloud webhooks)
- Implements the DataUpdateCoordinator
- Integration entry unloading
- Less "interesting" entities are disabled by default and can be enabled by the user.
- Simplified general structure

Issues/features in this PR (package perspective):

- The library used has been replaced
- Fully asynchronous implementation using aiohttp
- Respects API rate limit responses by backing off (on request of Quby)
- Does fewer requests overall
- Has support for registering/unregistering/handling webhooks
- Support passing in a method to handle token refreshes
- Support passing in an aiohttp client session

Some remarks:

- The key/unique IDs of entities may seem odd, but that is to make them backward compatible with the previous integration. People would lose history is that was changed.
- There is no migration path for the config entry, none of the data is useful or compatible. The migration done in this PR is by removing the entry and start a configuration flow to set up a new one.

I realize this PR is big, at the time I last touched this integration, I was not as involved with the project as I am now. Furthermore, a lot of UI stuff at the time was fairly new. The integration contained a lot of tech debt and stuff we would not accept anymore these days.

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/195327/85205532-2afe8180-b31c-11ea-8028-fe7e8bbcd0a8.png)


## Type of change
<!--
  What type of change does your PR introduce to Home Assistant?
  NOTE: Please, check only 1! box! 
  If your PR requires multiple boxes to be checked, you'll most likely need to
  split it into multiple PRs. This makes things easier and faster to code review.
-->

- [ ] New integration (thank you!)
- [x] New feature (which adds functionality to an existing integration)
- [x] Breaking change (fix/feature causing existing functionality to break)
- [x] Code quality improvements to existing code or addition of tests

-->
